messages.content: Does increasing ram affects vram?

No, increasing RAM does not directly affect VRAM. RAM and VRAM are two different types of memory used for different purposes in a computer. RAM is used for general system operations and running programs, while VRAM is specifically dedicated to storing and rendering graphics, such as in video games or 3D modeling applications. However, having more RAM can indirectly improve graphics performance by allowing the CPU to process more data and freeing up resources for the GPU to utilize.
How can I improve vram

Unfortunately, VRAM cannot be increased or upgraded on a computer unless you replace the graphics card with a new one that has more VRAM. However, there are a few things you can do to optimize your current VRAM:

1. Lower graphics settings: Reducing graphics settings in games or other applications can help reduce VRAM usage.

2. Close unnecessary programs: Closing any unnecessary programs running in the background can free up VRAM for more demanding applications.

3. Upgrade the graphics card: If your computer is using an older graphics card, upgrading to a newer one can provide more VRAM and better performance.

4. Use a lower resolution: Lowering the resolution of the display can reduce the amount of VRAM required for rendering graphics.

5. Use texture compression: Some applications support texture compression which can reduce VRAM usage without significantly affecting visual quality.
Does vram borrow from ssd

No, VRAM (Video Random Access Memory) does not borrow from SSD (Solid State Drive) or any other type of storage. VRAM is dedicated video memory that is integrated into the graphics card, and is used specifically for storing and rendering graphics. SSD, on the other hand, is a type of storage used for storing data and files, and it is not used for graphics processing. 

However, some applications and games may use system RAM as a backup for VRAM when there is not enough VRAM available. This is known as "shared memory" or "dynamic memory allocation". In this case, when the VRAM on the graphics card is full, it may use some of the RAM as temporary storage for graphics data. But this is not borrowing from SSD.
How to increase shared memory

Shared memory is a portion of the system RAM that is allocated for use as video memory when the dedicated VRAM on the graphics card is full. The amount of shared memory allocated is usually set up automatically by the computer's BIOS or UEFI firmware based on the amount of RAM installed in the system and the capabilities of the integrated graphics processor (if present). In most cases, it's not possible to manually increase the amount of shared memory beyond what the firmware settings allow. 

However, you can try the following steps to increase the available shared memory:

1. Boot into your computer's BIOS or UEFI firmware setup (usually done by pressing a specific key during system startup, such as F2, F10, or Delete).

2. Look for a setting related to "video" or "graphics". Depending on your system, these settings may be found under different headings or menus.

3. Once you find the video or graphics settings, look for an option that allows you to adjust the amount of shared memory. Note that not all systems will have this option available.

4. If you find the shared memory option, adjust it to the desired value and save your changes before exiting the firmware setup.

Keep in mind that increasing shared memory may have a negative impact on overall system performance as it reduces the amount of RAM available for other system tasks.
If I’ll increase ram will it also increase shared memory

Increasing the amount of RAM in your computer will not directly increase the amount of shared memory available for use as video memory. However, it can indirectly improve the performance of shared memory. 

When there is not enough dedicated video memory (VRAM) available, the system will use a portion of the main system RAM as shared memory (or integrated video memory) for the graphics card. So, with more RAM available, more memory will be available for the system to use as shared memory. 

However, increasing the amount of RAM available for shared memory also depends on the computer's BIOS or UEFI firmware settings. The amount of shared memory that can be allocated is usually set automatically by the firmware based on the amount of RAM installed on the system and the capabilities of the integrated video card. Some systems allow you to manually adjust the amount of shared memory available, as I mentioned earlier, but not all systems have this option available.
Does ASUS allow it

Asus computers have different BIOS or UEFI firmware settings depending on the model and the specific configuration. Some Asus computers may allow you to manually adjust the amount of shared memory available, while others may not. 

To check if your Asus computer allows you to adjust the amount of shared memory, follow these steps:

1. Boot your computer and enter the BIOS or UEFI firmware settings. Depending on the model, you may need to press a specific key or combination of keys during startup, such as F2, F10, or Delete.

2. Look for the video, graphics, or chipset-related settings in the BIOS or UEFI menus.

3. Check if there is a setting related to "iGPU memory" or "shared memory". If there is such a setting, it allows you to adjust the amount of shared memory.

4. If there is such a setting, you can try increasing the amount of shared memory to see if it helps with performance. Keep in mind that increasing shared memory may reduce the amount of RAM available for other system tasks, potentially affecting overall system performance.

If you cannot find a setting for adjusting shared memory in the BIOS or UEFI menus, your Asus computer may not allow you to do so.
Does increasing shared memory increase gaming performance

Increasing shared memory can improve gaming performance only if your computer's dedicated video memory (VRAM) is fully allocated and causing a bottleneck in performance. Shared memory is a portion of the system RAM that can be used as video memory when the dedicated VRAM runs out, but it's much slower and not as efficient as dedicated VRAM. So, when the VRAM is not fully utilized, allocating more shared memory will not improve gaming performance.

However, if you have an integrated graphics processor (IGP) with limited VRAM, increasing shared memory may allow more intensive games to be played. Note that increasing shared memory will use up more of your system's RAM which may have a negative impact on overall system performance. 

In general, the best way to improve gaming performance is to upgrade your graphics card to one with more VRAM, rather than relying on shared memory. Dedicated VRAM is specifically designed for graphics processing, which makes it faster and more efficient than shared memory.
What games can iris se 12th gen graphics can play

The 12th Gen Intel Core processors feature the latest Intel Iris Xe Graphics, which are significantly more powerful than previous generations. Iris Xe graphics are capable of playing many modern games at acceptable frame rates, although the exact performance will depend on the specific processor model, amount of RAM installed, and the game settings.

Here are some popular games that the Iris Xe graphics can play:

1. Fortnite
2. League of Legends
3. Overwatch
4. Valorant
5. Rocket League
6. Counter-Strike: Global Offensive
7. Minecraft
8. Among Us
9. Fall Guys: Ultimate Knockout
10. Apex Legends

Note that the performance of the processor and graphics will vary depending on the game's requirements, the resolution and the settings you select. In general, games that are not graphically intensive or do not require high frame rates should run smoothly on Iris Xe graphics.
I have i5 1235u and 20gb ram with that

The Intel Core i5-1235U is a 10th generation processor with 4 cores and 8 threads, a base clock speed of 1.7GHz, and a Turbo Boost of up to 3.7GHz. With 20GB of RAM, your system should have plenty of memory for most tasks.

The Iris Xe graphics integrated into the i5-1235U processor are capable of running many games, but the specific performance will depend on the game's requirements and the settings used. The CPU and GPU combination of the i5-1235U and Iris Xe graphics should allow you to play many modern games at lower settings and resolutions. Here are some games that you may be able to play:

1. Fortnite
2. League of Legends
3. Overwatch
4. Valorant
5. Rocket League
6. Counter-Strike: Global Offensive
7. Minecraft
8. Among Us

Note that the performance will vary depending on the specific model of i5-1235U you have, the graphics settings used, and the resolution of your display. However, with 20GB of RAM and the Iris Xe graphics, your system should be capable of running many popular games at lower settings.
No it’s a 12th generation processor

My apologies for the earlier mistake.

The 12th Generation Intel Core processors are the latest processors from Intel and are designed to offer excellent performance in a variety of applications, including gaming. The i5-1235U with its 4 cores and 8 threads, base clock speed of 2.9GHz, and turbo boost up to 4.2GHz should be able to handle most modern games. 

With the 20GB of RAM that you have, your system should be able to handle multitasking and running several applications simultaneously along with gaming.

Some games that you should be able to play at decent settings and good frame rates with Iris Xe graphics of i5-1235U are:

1. Fortnite
2. Rocket League
3. Valorant
4. Among Us
5. CS:GO
6. Overwatch

Note that the actual performance will depend on factors such as the settings you choose in the game, the resolution of your display, and the amount of VRAM that is available to your system. Nonetheless, with the latest 12th Gen Intel Iris Xe graphics, you should be able to run most modern games at medium graphics settings and respectable frame rates.
No it has 10 cores and 12 threads

I apologize for the confusion in my previous messages. The i5-1235U is actually not a 12th generation processor, but a 10th generation processor with 4 cores and 8 threads. I'm sorry for the mistake.

As for a processor with 10 cores and 12 threads, the closest match would be the Intel Core i9-10900K processor, which is a 10th generation processor. The i9-10900K has a base clock speed of 3.7GHz and a turbo boost of up to 5.3GHz, and it's one of the most powerful processors available in the 10th generation lineup.

With such a powerful processor, you should be able to handle most modern games without any issues. You'll also need a good graphics card to get the best performance in gaming. Here are some games that you should be able to run in high settings with a good graphics card:

1. Battlefield V
2. Call of Duty: Modern Warfare
3. Control
4. Doom Eternal
5. Red Dead Redemption 2
6. Shadow of the Tomb Raider
7. Cyberpunk 2077

Once again, the actual performance that you'll get in each of these games will depend on the graphics settings that you choose, the resolution of your display, and the performance of your graphics card.
No bro it’s an intel i5 12th gen processor with 10 cores and 12 threads

I apologize for my previous mistakes. To answer your question, there is no i5 processor in the 12th generation, so I'm not sure which processor you are referring to. The 12th generation Intel processors are the latest processors from Intel, and so far, only the i9 and the i7 processors have been announced.

The i5 processors are usually mid-range processors, with fewer cores and threads than the i7 and i9 processors. However, if you have a 12th generation i5 with 10 cores and 12 threads, it would be an impressive processor that could handle most tasks with ease, including high-end gaming.

Some games that you can play with such a powerful processor are:

1. Red Dead Redemption 2
2. Cyberpunk 2077
3. Control
4. Gears 5
5. Assassin's Creed Valhalla
6. Call of Duty: Modern Warfare
7. Battlefield V

Note that the actual gaming performance of the processor would depend on several factors, including the graphics card, the amount of RAM, and the quality of the display. Nonetheless, with such a powerful processor, you should be able to run most modern games with high graphics settings and good frame rates.
